title: Detroit · The Long Fall
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2016 + DPD RMS 2017–2026 · 205 neighborhoods · real locations

hook.line: Detroit crime, from the 1985 peak to 2016
hook.sub: FBI UCR · then the map, month by month, up close

punchline.sub: −69% under the same FBI measure · a newer, broader count begins in 2017

copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2016 the gray bars are the FBI's classic 'index' crimes (murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft). From 2017 the figures are DPD's own records-management system, which logs every reported incident — a broader net, so the level steps up. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · DPD RMS
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location, block-level, sampled.
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 205 neighborhoods — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level). 99.1% map to one of 205 neighborhoods — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2016) and DPD RMS incidents (2017+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Detroit neighborhood reports the fewest crimes?
copy.sourceLine: Data: Detroit Police Department (RMS) via the City of Detroit Open Data Portal · real block-level locations
copy.coverageText: 99.1% of records mapped to a neighborhood
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Detroit Police Department incident data (2017–present) via the City of Detroit Open Data Portal & FBI UCR (1985–2016) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer (ORI MI8234900). Neighborhoods: City of Detroit official layer. FBI UCR index crimes and DPD's incident records are different measures, shown as two chapters and never equated. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1985: the peak — 149,954 index crimes, at the depth of Detroit's crisis.
By 2000 the total is 97,776 — already a third below the peak.
2016: 46,048 — down 69% from 1985; the DPD incident record begins next year.
2023: 81,696 reported incidents — the high point of the newer, broader count.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — anonymized to the block by DPD open data.
Downtown — Detroit's busiest neighborhood for reported crime over the last five years.
August 2024 — the busiest month for reported crime in the last five years: 7,385.
State Fair: down 55% since 2022 — the city's biggest neighborhood drop.
Reported crime has held roughly flat: 75,248 (2022) → 73,634 (2025).
